ops.title: ccx vcs ship's \"pushed X → origin\" line may name a sibling bookmark parked at @'s tip, not main — check jj bookmark list before bookmark surgery
ops.body:
In cc-notes (2026-07-13), `ccx vcs ship` reported "pushed holder-v2-ccnotes → origin" for a commit that was in fact also landed on `main` — both bookmarks were parked at the same tip and both advanced (local and @origin) to the new commit. The one-line ship output names only one of them.

**Why:** concurrent sessions leave extra bookmarks (e.g. `holder-v2-ccnotes`) at main's tip; ship moves what's at @'s lineage and its report undersells the full set.

**How to apply:** when ship's output names an unexpected bookmark, run `jj bookmark list --all` and check where `main@origin` actually points before any bookmark move/rollback — the push usually did include main. Related: [[verify-lineage-before-ship]], [[ship-index-lock-race-rolls-back-bookmark]]. Side effect worth flagging to the user: the foreign bookmark fast-forwards too.
